teaching_card.title: Mean, Median, and Range (Easy)
teaching_card.body: The mean is the sum divided by the count. The median is the middle value when data are in order. The range is the largest value minus the smallest. Each summarizes a data set differently.
key_points: Median = middle value (order the data first).

screens.title: Center: mean, median, mode
screens.body: Mean is the average (sum ÷ count). Median is the middle value when the data is sorted (average the two middle ones if the count is even). Mode is the most frequent value.
key_points: Median = middle of the sorted list; mode = most frequent.

screens.title: Spread: range
screens.body: Range measures how spread out the data is: greatest − least. It's a quick measure of spread and is easy to confuse with the other statistics, so read the question word carefully.
key_points: Don't mix up range with mean or median.

screens.title: Work backward from the mean
screens.body: If you know the mean and the count, you know the TOTAL (mean × count). That lets you find a missing value: total minus the values you do know gives the one you don't.
key_points: Missing value = total − (sum of the known values).

screens.title: Try the moves
screens.body: Mean of 4, 6, 8, 10, 12 is 40 ÷ 5 = 8; median is 8; range is 12 − 4 = 8. If five numbers average 10 and four are 8, 9, 11, 12 (sum 40), the fifth is 50 − 40 = 10.
